body.title: Audit dashboard/metrics — every number from a real, active source
body.result: Full audit: all metrics verified live + accurate. Removed misleading 'Graph: 0%' (tier.1 count, always zero). Report at ~/.TheIgors/akien/inbox/dashboard_metrics_audit.md.
body.description:
Review every number shown in Igor's dashboard and metrics commands. Ensure each value comes from a real, active data source — no stale caches, no hardcoded defaults, no dead code paths.

## Scope
- Dashboard terminal display (boot banner, /introspect)
- get_stats() values sent to utility closet web UI
- /api/metrics endpoint
- build_report() sections in metrics.py

## For each metric, verify:
1. Where does the number come from? (DB query, in-memory counter, system call)
2. Is the source still active? (not a removed module, not a deprecated path)
3. Is it fresh? (not a stale cached value from hours ago)
4. Is it meaningful? (does it tell Igor or Akien something actionable)

## Known suspects
- Graph: 0% shown in boot log — is word graph usage actually measured?
- cloud_mode: OFF / cloud calls: 100% — contradictory or confusing labels
- MACHINES_EMPTY spam — machine_manager queried before register_self completes

## Related
- T-metrics-store (#411) — broader metrics infrastructure
- T-uc-comms-module — metrics logging may move to comms channels
